response.justification: A highly experienced and versatile engineer, but a poor fit for this specific role. The core requirement is modern C++/STL expertise for high-performance industrial systems, yet the candidate's C++ exposure is limited to ~7 months at National Instruments (2015) and a university thesis; C++ is not even listed in their skills. Their depth is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. There is no evidence of MQTT, Modbus/OPC UA, message brokers, IEC 62443/SDL secure engineering, or industrial automation. Adjacent strengths in low-latency distributed systems and multithreading exist but do not offset the central language and domain mismatch.
strengths: Deep, broad engineering experience (15+ years) with strong testing/TDD discipline
Proven low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems work (millions of req/s)
Multithreading/concurrency exposure via async I/O and fibers
concerns: Minimal and dated C++ experience (~7 months, 2015); C++ not in skill list
No industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL, MQTT, or OT-protocol background
No evidence of message-broker or event-driven architecture in C++
